description: Fix two CSS custom property values that diverge from the RetroUI source of truth: --muted in light mode (#aeaeae should be #d5d5d5) and --border in dark mode (#3a3a3a should be #5c5c5c). Both values are wrong in two files: docs global.css and side-panel styles.css.

userStories.title: Fix --muted and --border CSS tokens in docs global.css
userStories.description: As a user browsing the docs site, I want the muted color in light mode and border color in dark mode to exactly match the RetroUI design system so that the visual appearance is pixel-perfect.
acceptanceCriteria: No other CSS custom property values are changed
userStories.notes: File: docs/src/styles/global.css. Line 51: change `--muted: #aeaeae;` to `--muted: #d5d5d5;`. Line 78: change `--border: #3a3a3a;` to `--border: #5c5c5c;`. The RetroUI source of truth is ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/app/global.css lines 51 and 82.

userStories.title: Fix --muted and --border CSS tokens in side-panel styles.css
userStories.description: As a user viewing the Chrome extension side panel, I want the muted color in light mode and border color in dark mode to exactly match the RetroUI design system so that the visual appearance is pixel-perfect.
acceptanceCriteria: No other CSS custom property values are changed
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css. Line 33: change `--muted: #aeaeae;` to `--muted: #d5d5d5;`. Line 51: change `--border: #3a3a3a;` to `--border: #5c5c5c;`. The RetroUI source of truth is ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/app/global.css lines 51 and 82.
